frameworks.name: working memory neural substrate
frameworks.one_line: short-term storage and manipulation of task-relevant information across prefrontal and cortical regions

frameworks.name: noninvasive brain stimulation
frameworks.one_line: localized cortical perturbation to causally probe and modulate cognitive function

frameworks.name: executive control of behavior
frameworks.one_line: working memory framed as a component of goal-directed executive control

frameworks.name: stimulation combined with neuroimaging and electrophysiology
frameworks.one_line: concurrent recording during stimulation links perturbation to neural activity changes

core_question: What do brain-stimulation studies reveal about the neural substrate and mechanisms supporting working memory in humans?
key_assumptions: working memory comprises encoding, maintenance, and manipulation stages
prefrontal and other cortical regions causally support working memory
noninvasive stimulation can be localized enough to dissect cortical contributions
stimulation effects on cognition reflect underlying neural mechanisms
manipulations: noninvasive stimulation site across prefrontal and other cortical regions
combination of stimulation with neuroimaging or electrophysiological recording
stimulation applied during working-memory cognitive tasks
key_findings: brain stimulation can noninvasively modulate working-memory performance in humans
combined stimulation-recording methods reveal stimulation-linked neural activity changes
deficits in working memory accompany brain damage and neuropsychological disorders
interpretations: stimulation studies clarify the neural substrate and mechanisms underlying working memory
causal intervention complements correlational recording for understanding cognitive control
